title: Method for combustible mass recovery from bottom ash and installation for recovery of combustible mass from ash
abstract: The method for combustible mass recovery from bottom ash, primarily sourced from the burners of power boilers, that is stoker-fired, pulverized fuel or fluidized-bed boilers, especially those fired by fossil fuels, municipal, agricultural, animal or industrial waste or sludge is characterized in that the bottom ash with combustible mass and slag crushed to a size of less than 5 mm being screened through an array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes, while subsequent sieves are used to capture plus mesh of graining over 1000 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably over 800 ĂŽÂĽm and most preferably over 500 ĂŽÂĽm, which then constitutes the recovered combustible mass. The installation for the recovery of combustible mass from ash characterized in that it consists of multi-sieve vibrating screen (10) equipped with several screens, each mesh size in the range of 5000 ĂŽÂĽm to 500 ĂŽÂĽm, while the mesh size of the finest screen is in the range of 1000 to 500 ĂŽÂĽm and preferably 800 ĂŽÂĽm; moreover it has a chute connected through a feeder (9) with a fly ash storage area (8) and through another feeder (6) with a hopper of minus mesh of a vibrating screen (5) of a mesh size of 5000 ĂŽÂĽm to 2000 ĂŽÂĽm, a chute of which is connected through a feeder (4) with a hopper of the crusher (3), a chute of which is connected through a feeder (2) with a bottom ash storage area (1) and through another feeder (7) with a hopper of plus mesh of vibrating screen (5).
claims:
Claims Patent claims 1. The method of recovering combustible mass from bottom ash is distinctive by the bottom ash with combustible mass and slag crushed to a size of less than 5 mm being screened through an array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes, while subsequent sieves are used to capture plus mesh of graining over 1000 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably over 800 ĂŽÂĽm and most preferably over 500 ĂŽÂĽm, which then constitutes the recovered combustible mass. 2. The method according to claim 1 is distinctive by the bottom ash being crushed to a grain size of less than 2 mm. 3. The method according to claim 1 is distinctive by each sieve in the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes having a mesh size range between 5000 ĂŽÂĽm and 500 ĂŽÂĽm, with the plus mesh of each sieve forming the recovered combustible mass. 4. The method according to claim 1 or 3 is distinctive by the finest sieve of the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes having a mesh size between 1000 ĂŽÂĽm and 500 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably 800 ĂŽÂĽm. 5. The method according to claim 1, 3 or 4 is distinctive by the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes including two or three sieves with a mesh size range between 1500 ĂŽÂĽm and 500 ĂŽÂĽm, while the mesh size difference between the sieves is between 200 ĂŽÂĽm and 500 ĂŽÂĽm, with the plus mesh of each sieve forming the recovered combustible mass. 6. The method according to claim 1, 3 or 4 is distinctive by the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes including three sieves with a mesh size range between 2000 ĂŽÂĽm and 500 ĂŽÂĽm, while the mesh size difference between sieves is at least 200 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably the mesh size is 1500 ĂŽÂĽm for the first sieve, 1000 ĂŽÂĽm for the second sieve, 800 ĂŽÂĽm or 500 ĂŽÂĽm for the third sieve, with the plus mesh of each sieve forming the recovered combustible mass. 7. The method according to claim 1, 3 or 4 is distinctive by the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes including four sieves with a mesh size range between 3000 ĂŽÂĽm and 500 ĂŽÂĽm, while the mesh size difference between sieves is at 

 least 200 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably the mesh size is 3000 ĂŽÂĽĂŹÂ„ĂŽÂ± for the first sieve, 2000 ĂŽÂĽm for the second sieve, 1000 ĂŽÂĽm for the third sieve, 800 ĂŽÂĽĂŽÂ·ĂŽÂą or 500 ĂŽÂĽm for the fourth sieve, with the plus mesh of each sieve forming the recovered combustible mass. 8. The method according to claim 1, 3 or 4 is distinctive by the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes consisting of sieves with the mesh sizes of SOOO ĂŽÂĽm, 3000 ĂŽÂĽm, 2000 ĂŽÂĽm, 1000 ĂŽÂĽm and the last, finest sieve with the mesh size in the range from 900 ĂŽÂĽm to 500 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably 800 ĂŽÂĽm, with the plus mesh of each sieve forming the recovered combustible mass. 9. The method according to claim 1 or 2 is distinctive by bottom ash of a size of less than 5 mm, preferably less than 2 mm, being crushed in crusher (3), and before it is fed to the multi-sieve array with gradually decreasing mesh sizes it is screened in the sieve vibrating screen (5) with a mesh size between 5000 ĂŽÂĽm and 2000 ĂŽÂĽm, while the minus mesh of that sieve vibrating screen (5) is the bottom ash fed to the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes. 10. The method according to claim 9 is distinctive by the plus mesh of the sieve vibrating screen (5) being again fed for crushing to a size of less than 5 mm, preferably less than 2 mm, in crusher (3). 11. The method according to claim 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 is distinctive by the fly ash containing combustible mass, alternately with the bottom ash, being screened in the array of sieves with gradually decreasing mesh sizes, while the plus mesh separated at each sieve, of more than 1000 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably more than 800 ĂŽÂĽm and most preferably more than 500 ĂŽÂĽm, is the combustible mass recovered from that fly ash. 12. The method according to claim 1 is distinctive due to the fact that minus mesh from the array of several screens of a gradually decreasing mesh size is passed to ash storage area (13) or is passed after milling in the grinding mill (15) to the particle size below 0.5 mm to vibrating screen (16) of the mesh size of the finest screen in range of 250 to 80 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably 200 ĂŽÂĽm, from which minus mesh is directed to a dusty ash storage area (17) and plus mesh to a low calorific value combustible mass storage area (19). 13. The method according to claim 1 is distinctive due to the fact that plus mesh containing recovered combustible mass from subsequent screens of the array of several screens of a gradually decreasing mesh size is taken to a high calorific value combustible mass storage area (22). 14. The installation for the recovery of combustible mass from ash characterized in that 

 it consists of multi-sieve vibrating screen (10) equipped with several screens, each mesh size in the range of 5000 urn to 500 ĂŽÂĽm, while the mesh size of the finest screen is in the range of 1000 to 500 ĂŽÂĽm and preferably 800 ĂŽÂĽm; moreover it has a chute connected through a feeder (9) with a fly ash storage area (8) and through another feeder (6) with a hopper of minus mesh of a vibrating screen (5) of a mesh size of 5000 ĂŽÂĽm to 2000 ĂŽÂĽm, a chute of which is connected through a feeder (4) with a hopper of the crusher (3), a chute of which is connected through a feeder (2) with a bottom ash storage area (1) and through another feeder (7) with a hopper of plus mesh of vibrating screen (5). 15. The installation according to claim 14 characterized in that a hopper of plus mesh of multi-sieve vibrating screen (10) is connecting through a feeder (24) with a high calorific value combustible mass tray (20) and then through another feeder (21) with high calorific value combustible mass storage area (22), while a hopper of minus mesh is connected through a feeder (12) with an ash storage area (13) and through a subsequent feeder (14) with a grinding mill (15) connected through a feeder (23) with a vibrating screen (16) with the finest screen mesh size in the range 250 to 80 ĂŽÂĽm, preferably 200 ĂŽÂĽm, whose a plus mesh hopper is connected through a feeder (18) with a low calorific value combustible mass storage area (19), while a hopper of minus mesh is connected through a feeder (25) with dusty ash storage area (17). 16. The installation according to claim 14 characterized in that multi-sieve vibrating screen (10) is equipped with an array of five screens with mesh sizes equal to 5000, 3000 , 2000, 1000 and 800 ĂŽÂĽm or 500 ĂŽÂĽm. 17. The installation according to claim 15 characterized in that multi-sieve vibrating screen (1 ) is equipped with an array of three screens with mesh sizes equal to 500, 200, 100 or two screens with mesh size equal to 500 ĂŽÂĽm and 200 ĂŽÂĽm. 18. The installation according to claim 15 characterized in that a high calorific value combustible mass storage area (22) is connected through a feeder (26) with a briquetting or pelletizing device (27). 19. The installation according to claim 15 characterized in that a high calorific value combustible mass storage area (22) is connected through a feeder (28) with equipment for activated carbon production (29). 20. The installation according to claim 15 characterized in that a high calorific value combustible mass storage area (22) is connected through a feeder (30) with a coal 

 dust mill (31) connected with a nozzle burner (32) and a powdered-fuel burner (33) of a power boiler (37). 21. The installation according to claim IS characterized in that a low calorific value combustible mass storage area (19) and a high calorific value combustible mass storage area (22) are interconnected through associated feeders (34, 35) with a coal dust mill (36) connected with a powdered-fuel burner (33) of a power boiler (37).
